title: SpiceAI — Ingredient-Based Recipe Recommendation System with Data Analytics

requirements.text: Build a full-page React application titled SpiceAI — Ingredient-Based Recipe Recommendation System with Data Analytics. The app must have a hero header section with the SpiceAI brand name (with a curry bowl emoji), the full project subtitle INGREDIENT-BASED RECIPE RECOMMENDATION SYSTEM WITH DATA ANALYTICS, and a visually rich background using the hero banner image (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png) as a backdrop with a warm gradient overlay.

quotes: my project title is INGREDIENT-BASED RECIPE RECOMMENDATION SYSTEM WITH DATA ANALYTICS

acceptanceCriteria: Header displays SpiceAI as the brand name with a curry bowl emoji
Full project subtitle is shown below the brand name
Hero section uses the generated banner image with warm gradient overlay
Header is visually prominent and polished

requirements.text: Implement an ingredient search section with a styled input field where users can type ingredients separated by commas (e.g., tomato, onion, paneer). Include a prominent Find Recipes button. When submitted, call the Spoonacular API: GET https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/findByIngredients?ingredients={ingredients}&number=5&cuisine=Indian&apiKey=f1a04579d22649258b5b03a04d119c6f. Show a loading spinner with text 'Cooking delicious Indian recipes...' while the request is in progress. If the input is empty, show a validation message.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field accepts comma-separated ingredients
Find Recipes button triggers the search
Loading animation displays during API call
Empty input shows a friendly validation message
If no recipes found, display a No Indian recipes found message

requirements.text: Display recipe search results as a responsive grid of recipe cards. Each card must show: the recipe image, recipe title, a green badge for number of used ingredients, an orange badge for number of missing ingredients, and a View Recipe button. Cards must have hover lift/scale effects. Use React Query to manage API fetch state.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipes render in a responsive grid of 2 to 4 columns depending on viewport
Each card shows image, title, used count badge, and missing count badge
View Recipe button opens the recipe detail view
Cards have smooth hover scale animation

requirements.text: Implement a recipe detail view. When View Recipe is clicked, fetch from: GET https://api.spoonacular.com/recipes/{id}/information?includeNutrition=true&apiKey=f1a04579d22649258b5b03a04d119c6f. Display: recipe title, image, a nutrition panel with styled stat cards for Calories (kcal), Protein (g), Fat (g), and Carbohydrates (g), a full ingredient list, and numbered step-by-step cooking instructions. Include a Back to Results button to return to the recipe list.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail view shows title, image, 4 nutrition stat cards, ingredient list, and numbered instructions
Back button returns to the recipe results list without re-fetching
Nutrition values are correctly extracted from the API nutrition.nutrients array

requirements.text: Add a Data Analytics section below the recipe results. Render a bar chart comparing Calorie content (kcal) for each recipe viewed in the current session, labelled Ingredient Analytics — Calorie Comparison. Also render a doughnut chart showing the macronutrient breakdown (Protein, Fat, Carbohydrates) for the most recently viewed recipe. Use Recharts or react-chartjs-2. Both charts must be styled with the app color theme and update dynamically as the user views more recipes.
acceptanceCriteria: Bar chart displays calorie values for all recipes viewed in the current session
Doughnut chart shows Protein, Fat, and Carbs breakdown for the last viewed recipe
Both charts update dynamically without page reload
Analytics section has a clear heading and styled card container

requirements.text: Apply a warm Indian-cuisine-inspired visual theme across the entire application. Use a saffron-to-deep-crimson color palette (primary #E63946, accent #F4A261, warm background #FFF8F0, secondary green #2D6A4F). Load Poppins font from Google Fonts for all text. Use rounded cards with soft shadows, generous spacing, and smooth transitions. Set a full-width layout with a centered 1200px max-width container. Apply the spice-pattern texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png) as a subtle repeating tile on section backgrounds.

quotes: kindly make a beautiful attractive website

acceptanceCriteria: Saffron and crimson color palette is consistently applied site-wide
Poppins font is loaded and applied to all headings and body text
All cards use rounded corners and soft drop shadows
Layout uses a centered max-width container of 1200px
Section backgrounds use the spice pattern as a subtle tile texture
All interactive elements have smooth hover and transition animations

requirements.text: Render the generated hero banner image (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png) and spice pattern texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png) and logo (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png) as static assets served from frontend/public/assets/generated. Reference them in the hero header background, section background tiling, and navbar logo respectively.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero banner image is displayed as the header section background
Spice pattern texture tiles across content section backgrounds
Logo image renders in the navbar
All images load from the /assets/generated/ path

requirements.text: Add a sticky navigation bar at the top with the SpiceAI logo (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png) on the left and anchor links to Search, Recipes, and Analytics sections on the right. The navbar should start semi-transparent with a frosted-glass effect and transition to a solid warm background color on scroll.

quotes: kindly make a beautiful attractive website

acceptanceCriteria: Navbar is sticky and always visible during scroll
Logo image renders on the left side of the navbar
Anchor links smoothly scroll to their respective sections
Navbar background transitions from frosted/transparent to solid on scroll

constraints: The Spoonacular API key must be exactly: f1a04579d22649258b5b03a04d119c6f
All Spoonacular API calls must be made directly from the frontend — do not proxy through the Motoko backend
No backend state storage is required for this feature set
nonGoals: User authentication or saved recipe history persistence
Backend recipe caching or storage in Motoko

required: Wide cinematic hero banner showing a vibrant spread of colorful Indian spices, fresh vegetables including tomato onion paneer and coriander, and decorative copper cooking pots on a warm wooden surface with rich warm golden lighting (spiceai-hero.dim_1440x500.png)

Seamless repeating tile pattern of small Indian spice illustrations including mustard seeds chili peppers cumin and coriander leaves on a warm cream background suitable as a subtle texture (spice-pattern.dim_400x400.png)
Horizontal wordmark logo with a small stylized chili pepper icon beside bold text SpiceAI on a transparent background using saffron and crimson tones (spiceai-logo.dim_120x40.png)
